0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("GZDWDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("JZDZ")), substrb(dump(min("JZDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("JZDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("HKDZ")), substrb(dump(min("HKDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("HKDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("HKDZYB")), substrb(dump(min("HKDZYB"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("HKDZYB"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count( "LXRXM")), substrb(dump(min("LXRXM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("LXRXM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("LXRGX")), substrb(dump(min("LXRGX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("LXRGX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("LXRDZ")), substrb(dump(min("LXRDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("LXRDZ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("JKKH")), substrb(dump(min("JKKH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("JKKH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("FKDQ")), substrb(dump(min("FKDQ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("FKDQ"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("XB")), substrb(dump(min("XB"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("XB"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("XM")), substrb(dump(min("XM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("XM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("HZLX")), substrb(dump(min("HZLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("HZLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("RID")), substrb(dump(min("RID"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("RID"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("CSDAH")), substrb(dump(min("CSDAH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("CSDAH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("DAH")), substrb(dump(min("DAH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("DAH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("WSJGDM")), substrb(dump(min("WSJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("WSJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("KH")), substrb(dump(min("KH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("KH"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("KLX")), substrb(dump(min("KLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("KLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("ZJHM")), substrb(dump(min("ZJHM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("ZJHM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("ZJLX")), substrb(dump(min("ZJLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("ZJLX"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("FJ_WSJGDM")), substrb(dump(min("FJ_WSJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("FJ_WSJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), to_char(count("YLJGDM")), substrb(dump(min("YLJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), substrb(dump(max("YLJGDM"), 16, 0, 64), 1, 240), count(rowidtochar(rowid)) from "CEN_CORE_V30"."TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X" t /* NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, ACL, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, NDV, NIL, NIL, TOPN, NIL, NIL, RWID, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254, U254U*/
csh4wxytvw7gnUPDATE sys. aclmvrefstat$ SET total_inserts = :1, total_deletes = :2, total_updates = :3 WHERE acl_mview_obj# = :4 AND status = -555
d13rgaub7kt2qSELECT :B8 AS JGDM, :B7 AS JGMC, :B6 AS QXDM, :B5 AS QXMC, :B4 AS GP_GH, :B3 AS GP_XM, :B2 AS XM, :B1 AS ZJHM, :B11 AS RWID, :B10 AS TXNR, '1' AS ZT, SYSDATE AS SCSJ, NULL AS TXSJ, NULL AS CLSJ, '1' AS XGBZ, T1.SFJHBH AS SOURCE_KEY FROM CDC_FR_MBDJ T JOIN CDC_TB_SFJH T1 ON T.MBGLBH = T1.MBGLBH AND T.STATUS = '0' AND T1.STATUS = '0' AND T1.SFBH IS NULL WHERE T.ZJHM = :B1 AND T.GLZT = '1' AND T.ZJLX = '01' AND TRUNC(:B9 ) BETWEEN TRUNC(ADD_MONTHS(T1.SFJZ_RQ, -1)) AND TRUNC(T1.SFJZ_RQ) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TB_YW_TXRWB RW WHERE RW.ZJHM = :B1 AND RWID = :B11 AND SOURCE_KEY = T1.SFJHBH )
d58zhx5m0kqz7SELECT :B8 AS JGDM, :B7 AS JGMC, :B6 AS QXDM, :B5 AS QXMC, :B4 AS GP_GH, :B3 AS GP_XM, :B2 AS XM, :B1 AS ZJHM, :B11 AS RWID, :B10 AS TXNR, '1' AS ZT, SYSDATE AS SCSJ, NULL AS TXSJ, NULL AS CLSJ, '1' AS XGBZ, T1.SFJHBH AS SOURCE_KEY FROM CDC_FR_MBDJ T JOIN CDC_TB_SFJH T1 ON T.MBGLBH = T1.MBGLBH AND T.STATUS = '0' AND T1.STATUS = '0' AND T1.SFBH IS NULL WHERE T.ZJHM = :B1 AND T.GLZT = '1' AND T.ZJLX = '01' AND TRUNC(:B9 ) BETWEEN TRUNC(T1.SFKS_RQ) AND TRUNC(T1.SFJZ_RQ) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TB_YW_TXRWB RW WHERE RW.ZJHM = :B1 AND RWID = :B11 AND SOURCE_KEY=T1.SFJHBH )
dgr17qvcg43wnselect t.* from doprocess t where t.interface =:1
dgw4rzu64up66begin MERGE INTO CEN_CORE_V30.TB_LIS_BACTERIA_RESULT a USING (SELECT SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, XJJGLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, XJDH, XJMC, JLJS, BYJ, BYSJ, PYTJ, FXFS, JCJG, JCJGWZ, jlscrq, posid FROM cen_interface_v30.TB_LIS_BACTERIA_RESULT c WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id ) b ON (A.XJJGLSH=B.XJJGLSH and A.YLJGDM=B.YLJGDM) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a.SBBM= b.SBBM, a.YQBH= b.YQBH, a.YQMC= b.YQMC, a.XGBZ= b.XGBZ, a.YLYL1= b.YLYL1, a.YLYL2= b.YLYL2, a.WSJGDM= b.WSJGDM, a.FJ_WSJGDM= b.FJ_WSJGDM, a.BGDH= b.BGDH, a.BGRQ= b.BGRQ, a.XJDH= b.XJDH, a.XJMC= b.XJMC, a.JLJS= b.JLJS, a.BYJ= b.BYJ, a.BYSJ= b.BYSJ, a.PYTJ= b.PYTJ, a.FXFS= b.FXFS, a.JCJG= b.JCJG, a.JCJGWZ= b.JCJGWZ , a.jlscrq= b.jlscrq, a.posid= b.posid, a.jlgxsj = sysdate WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT(SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, XJJGLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, XJDH, XJMC, JLJS, BYJ, BYSJ, PYTJ, FXFS, JCJG, JCJGWZ, jlscrq, posid) VALUES( b.SBBM, b.YQBH, b.YQMC, b.XGBZ, b.YLYL1, b.YLYL2, b.XJJGLSH, b.WSJGDM, b.FJ_WSJGDM, b.YLJGDM, b.BGDH, b.BGRQ, b.XJDH, b.XJMC, b.JLJS, b.BYJ, b.BYSJ, b.PYTJ, b.FXFS, b.JCJG, b.JCJGWZ, b.jlscrq, b.posid); DELETE FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_LIS_BACTERIA_RESULT b WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id; END;
dp35n3x99guar/* MV_REFRESH (DEL) */ delete from "CEN_ETL_JYTX"."TB_YY_PATIENTINFO"
dq3cx84f7802hMERGE INTO CENDOC.TB_YL_MEDICAL_RECORD_EXTEND A USING (SELECT DISTINCT JZLSH, YLJGDM, '1' AS YZMXB, KH, KLX FROM CENCORE.TB_CIS_PRESCRIPTION_DETAIL WHERE KH = :B2 AND KLX = :B1 ) B ON (A.JZLSH = B.JZLSH AND A.YLJGDM = B.YLJGDM) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.YZMXB = '1' WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (A.JZLSH, A.YLJGDM, A.YZMXB) VALUES (B.JZLSH, B.YLJGDM, B.YZMXB)
drpm88g74pvcjSELECT COUNT(*) FROM TB_JM_0022 T WHERE T.JM_ZJHM = :B1 AND T.RWID = '12' AND T.CLZT = '0'
dtkp3bvajm1a5MERGE/*+ append nologging parallel(32) */ INTO cen_core_ehr.tb_resident_basic_info a USING ( WITH frequency_records AS ( SELECT /*+ parallel(32) */ upper(pix.zjhm) AS id_number, '01' AS id_type, trim(pix.xm) AS name, pix.xb as gender, pix.csrq AS birth_date, --pix.hkdz AS native_place, pix.csd AS birthplace, pix.mz AS ethnicity, pix.CCRKSJ as create_date, pix.jlgxsj, pix.sjhm, pix.dhhm, -- ĽĆËă·ÇżŐÖµµÄłöĎÖĆµÂĘ COUNT(CASE WHEN trim(pix.xm) IS NOT NULL THEN trim(pix.xm) END) OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ) AS name_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN pix.xb IS NOT NULL THEN pix.xb END) OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ) AS gender_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN pix.csrq IS NOT NULL THEN pix.csrq END) OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ) AS birth_date_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN pix.csd IS NOT NULL THEN pix.csd END) OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ) AS birthplace_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN pix.mz IS NOT NULL THEN pix.mz END) OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ) AS ethnicity_count, -- ÓĂÓÚĆ˝ľÖĘ±ŃˇÔń×îĐÂĽÇÂĽ ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( PARTITION BY upper(pix.zjhm), pix.zjlx ORDER BY pix.jlgxsj DESC ) AS rec_rn FROM cen_core_v30.tb_patient_information_x pix WHERE pix.jlgxsj > to_date('20180101', 'yyyymmdd') AND pix.ZJHM IS NOT NULL AND (pix.ZJLX = '01' or pix.zjlx = '1') AND pix.XGBZ = '1' ), /*with latest_records as( select \*+ parallel(32) *\ sfzh as id_number, '01' AS id_type, jmxm as name, JMXB as gender, CSRQ as birth_date, '' as native_place, '' as birthplace, '' as ethnicity, jlsj as create_date, bgsj as jlgxsj, sjhm as sjhm, lxdh as dhhm, ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( PARTITION BY sfzh ORDER BY bgsj DESC ) AS rn from CEN_CORE_JYGL.T_SQZG_GPQY_QYXX where bgsj > vtime and QYSTATE = '1' ), */ most_frequent AS ( SELECT/*+ parallel(32) */ id_number, id_type, -- ŃˇÔńłöĎÖ´ÎĘý×î¶ŕµÄĐŐĂűŁ¨ÎŢĘÓżŐÖµŁ¬ČçąűÓĐ¶ŕ¸öĎŕÍ¬´ÎĘýŁ¬Čˇ×îĐÂµÄŁ© MAX(CASE WHEN name_count > 0 THEN name END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY name_count DESC, rec_rn) AS name, -- ŃˇÔńłöĎÖ´ÎĘý×î¶ŕµÄĐÔ±đ MAX(CASE WHEN gender_count > 0 THEN gender END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY gender_count DESC, rec_rn) AS gender, -- ŃˇÔńłöĎÖ´ÎĘý×î¶ŕµÄłöÉúČŐĆÚ MAX(CASE WHEN birth_date_count > 0 THEN birth_date END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY birth_date_count DESC, rec_rn) AS birth_date, -- ŃˇÔńłöĎÖ´ÎĘý×î¶ŕµÄłöÉúµŘ MAX(CASE WHEN birthplace_count > 0 THEN birthplace END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY birthplace_count DESC, rec_rn) AS birthplace, -- ŃˇÔńłöĎÖ´ÎĘý×î¶ŕµÄĂń×ĺ MAX(CASE WHEN ethnicity_count > 0 THEN ethnicity END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY ethnicity_count DESC, rec_rn) AS ethnicity, -- Čˇ×îÍíµÄ´´˝¨Ę±ĽäşÍ¸üĐÂĘ±Ľä MAX(create_date) AS create_date, MAX(jlgxsj) AS max_jlgxsj, -- ´¦Ŕíµç»°ŁşŃˇÔń·ÇżŐÖµŁ¬ČçąűÓĐ¶ŕ¸öÔňČˇ×îĐÂµÄ MAX(sjhm) AS sjhm, MAX(dhhm) AS dhhm, -- ÓĂÓÚµç»°ĆµÂĘĽĆËăŁ¨ČçąűĐčŇŞ°´ĆµÂĘŃˇÔńµç»°Ł© MAX(CASE WHEN sjhm IS NOT NULL THEN sjhm END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY rec_rn) AS latest_sjhm, MAX(CASE WHEN dhhm IS NOT NULL THEN dhhm END) KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST ORDER BY rec_rn) AS latest_dhhm FROM frequency_records GROUP BY id_number, id_type ), filtered_records AS ( SELECT/*+ parallel(32) */ mf.id_number, mf.id_type, mf.name, CASE WHEN mf.gender IN ('0', '1', '2', '9') THEN mf.gender ELSE '0' END AS gender, mf.birth_date, --mf.native_place, mf.birthplace, mf.ethnicity, mf.create_date, mf.max_jlgxsj AS update_date, -- ÓĹĎČĘąÓĂĘÖ»úşĹŁ¬Ă»ÓĐÔňÓĂµç»° COALESCE(mf.latest_sjhm, mf.latest_dhhm) AS phone FROM most_frequent mf ) SELECT/*+ parallel(32) */ fr.id_number, fr.id_type, fr.name, fr.gender, fr.birth_date, --fr.native_place, fr.birthplace, fr.ethnicity, fr.create_date, fr.update_date, '310000' AS data_source, fr.phone, hai.int_archive_id FROM filtered_records fr INNER JOIN ( SELECT id_number, id_type, MAX(int_archive_id) AS int_archive_id FROM cen_core_ehr.tb_health_archive_index GROUP BY id_number, id_type ) hai ON fr.id_number = hai.id_number AND fr.id_type = hai.id_type WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM cen_core_ehr.tb_resident_basic_info ext WHERE ext.id_number = fr.id_number AND ext.id_type = fr.id_type AND ext.data_source <> '310000' ) ) m ON (a.id_number = m.id_number AND a.id_type = m.id_type) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a.int_archive_id = m.int_archive_id, a.name = m.name, a.gender = m.gender, a.birth_date = m.birth_date, --a.native_place = m.native_place, a.birthplace = m.birthplace, a.ethnicity = m.ethnicity, a.create_date = m.create_date, a.update_date = CASE WHEN a.update_date < m.update_date THEN m.update_date ELSE a.update_date END, a.phone = m.phone WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT ( id, int_archive_id, name, id_number, id_type, gender, birth_date, --native_place, birthplace, ethnicity, data_source, create_date, update_date, phone ) VALUES ( sys_guid(), m.int_archive_id, m.name, m.id_number, m.id_type, m.gender, m.birth_date, --m.native_place, m.birthplace, m.ethnicity, m.data_source, m.create_date, m.update_date, m.phone )
dvd8y5c1jf484SELECT t.INT_ARCHIVE_ID, t.ID_NUMBER, t.ID_TYPE, i.name FROM CEN_CORE_EHR.TB_HEALTH_ARCHIVE_INDEX t inner join cen_core_ehr.TB_RESIDENT_BASIC_INFO i on t.INT_ARCHIVE_ID = i.INT_ARCHIVE_ID WHERE t.ID_NUMBER=:1 AND t.ID_TYPE=:2
f0h5rpzmhju11select SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'SERVER_HOST'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'DB_UNIQUE_NAME'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'INSTANCE_NAME'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'INSTANCE'), STARTUP_TIME, SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'DB_DOMAIN'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'SERVICE_NAME') from v$instance
f8k2zk1wu94t9INSERT INTO TB_JM_0022_TMP SELECT * FROM TABLE(PKG_JTYS_DBRWSC_TEST.F_GET_DBRW_PARALLEL(CURSOR(SELECT /*+PARALLEL(64)*/* FROM V_QYDX_SS)))
fk11mjgad217zSELECT HZWYH||'@!@'|| CSRQ||'@!@'||XB FROM CS_JBXX WHERE KH=:1 AND KLX=:2
fz1r32jzgurb6SELECT :B8 AS JGDM, :B7 AS JGMC, :B6 AS QXDM, :B5 AS QXMC, :B4 AS GP_GH, :B3 AS GP_XM, :B1 AS XM, :B2 AS ZJHM, :B10 AS RWID, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(:B9 , '{0}', :B1 ), '{1}', TO_CHAR(M.RYSJ, 'yyyy-mm-dd')), '{2}', Y.YLJGMC), '{3}', M.CYKSMC) AS TXNR, '1' AS ZT, SYSDATE AS SCSJ, NULL AS TXSJ, NULL AS CLSJ, '1' AS XGBZ, T.ID AS SOURCE_KEY FROM T_FJZL_DBRW T INNER JOIN TB_YL_ZY_MEDICAL_RECORD M ON M.JZLSH = REGEXP_SUBSTR(T.YWZJ, '[^, ]+', 1, 1) AND M.YLJGDM = REGEXP_SUBSTR(T.YWZJ, '[^, ]+', 1, 2) LEFT JOIN V_DIC_JG_KZ Y ON M.YLJGDM = Y.YLJGDM WHERE T.SFZH = :B2 AND RWLX='300' AND STATE='0' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TB_YW_TXRWB RW WHERE RW.ZJHM = :B2 AND RWID = :B10 AND SOURCE_KEY= T.ID)
g35fnzpw5xq5kbegin MERGE INTO CEN_CORE_V30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS a USING (SELECT JYZBLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, YBSFDM, YBSFDMYB, JCZBDM, JCFF, JCZBMC, JCZBJG, LOINC, SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, CKZ, JLDW, YCTS, DYXH, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, jlscrq, posid FROM cen_interface_v30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS c WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id ) b ON (A.YLJGDM=B.YLJGDM and A.JYZBLSH=B.JYZBLSH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a.WSJGDM= b.WSJGDM, a.FJ_WSJGDM= b.FJ_WSJGDM, a.BGDH= b.BGDH, a.BGRQ= b.BGRQ, a.YBSFDM= b.YBSFDM, a.YBSFDMYB= b.YBSFDMYB, a.JCZBDM= b.JCZBDM, a.JCFF= b.JCFF, a.JCZBMC= b.JCZBMC, a.JCZBJG= b.JCZBJG, a.LOINC= b.LOINC, a.SBBM= b.SBBM, a.YQBH= b.YQBH, a.YQMC= b.YQMC, a.CKZ= b.CKZ, a.JLDW= b.JLDW, a.YCTS= b.YCTS, a.DYXH= b.DYXH, a.XGBZ= b.XGBZ, a.YLYL1= b.YLYL1, a.YLYL2= b.YLYL2 , a.jlscrq= b.jlscrq, a.posid= b.posid, a.jlgxsj = sysdate WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT(JYZBLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, YBSFDM, YBSFDMYB, JCZBDM, JCFF, JCZBMC, JCZBJG, LOINC, SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, CKZ, JLDW, YCTS, DYXH, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, jlscrq, posid) VALUES( b.JYZBLSH, b.WSJGDM, b.FJ_WSJGDM, b.YLJGDM, b.BGDH, b.BGRQ, b.YBSFDM, b.YBSFDMYB, b.JCZBDM, b.JCFF, b.JCZBMC, b.JCZBJG, b.LOINC, b.SBBM, b.YQBH, b.YQMC, b.CKZ, b.JLDW, b.YCTS, b.DYXH, b.XGBZ, b.YLYL1, b.YLYL2, b.jlscrq, b.posid); DELETE FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS b WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id; END;
g6ud9an4w5dzzDELETE FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS B WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
g82uyzx7jc762MERGE INTO CEN_CORE_V30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS A USING (SELECT JYZBLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, YBSFDM, YBSFDMYB, JCZBDM, JCFF, JCZBMC, JCZBJG, LOINC, SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, CKZ, JLDW, YCTS, DYXH, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, JLSCRQ, POSID FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_LIS_INDICATORS C WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1 ) B ON (A.YLJGDM=B.YLJGDM AND A.JYZBLSH=B.JYZBLSH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.WSJGDM= B.WSJGDM, A.FJ_WSJGDM= B.FJ_WSJGDM, A.BGDH= B.BGDH, A.BGRQ= B.BGRQ, A.YBSFDM= B.YBSFDM, A.YBSFDMYB= B.YBSFDMYB, A.JCZBDM= B.JCZBDM, A.JCFF= B.JCFF, A.JCZBMC= B.JCZBMC, A.JCZBJG= B.JCZBJG, A.LOINC= B.LOINC, A.SBBM= B.SBBM, A.YQBH= B.YQBH, A.YQMC= B.YQMC, A.CKZ= B.CKZ, A.JLDW= B.JLDW, A.YCTS= B.YCTS, A.DYXH= B.DYXH, A.XGBZ= B.XGBZ, A.YLYL1= B.YLYL1, A.YLYL2= B.YLYL2 , A.JLSCRQ= B.JLSCRQ, A.POSID= B.POSID, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT(JYZBLSH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, BGDH, BGRQ, YBSFDM, YBSFDMYB, JCZBDM, JCFF, JCZBMC, JCZBJG, LOINC, SBBM, YQBH, YQMC, CKZ, JLDW, YCTS, DYXH, XGBZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, JLSCRQ, POSID) VALUES( B.JYZBLSH, B.WSJGDM, B.FJ_WSJGDM, B.YLJGDM, B.BGDH, B.BGRQ, B.YBSFDM, B.YBSFDMYB, B.JCZBDM, B.JCFF, B.JCZBMC, B.JCZBJG, B.LOINC, B.SBBM, B.YQBH, B.YQMC, B.CKZ, B.JLDW, B.YCTS, B.DYXH, B.XGBZ, B.YLYL1, B.YLYL2, B.JLSCRQ, B.POSID)
gg2hzkhx22y6rwith basic as (select hai.int_archive_id, pix.kh, pix.klx from cen_core_ehr.tb_health_archive_index hai INNER JOIN cen_core_v30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X pix ON pix.zjhm = hai.id_number AND (pix.zjlx = hai.id_type or pix.zjlx = '1') where pix.xgbz = '1' and hai.int_archive_id = :1 group by hai.int_archive_id, pix.kh, pix.klx), mz as (SELECT a.jzlsh, COALESCE(d1.DMXMC, a.JZZDSM) AS label FROM cen_core_v30.TB_YL_MZ_Medical_Record a LEFT JOIN cen_core_v30.TB_CIS_Prescription_Detail b ON (a.YLJGDM = b.YLJGDM AND a.jzlsh = b.jzlsh and b.cxbz = '1') INNER JOIN basic ON (a.kh = basic.kh AND a.klx = basic.klx) LEFT JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT DMXDMZ, DMXMC FROM cen_dic.tmp_dic_disease_all) d1 ON a.JZZDBM = d1.DMXDMZ WHERE a.xgbz = '1' AND NOT REGEXP_LIKE(a.JZZDBM, '^(A|B|C|D[0-4][0-9]|F)') GROUP BY a.jzlsh, COALESCE(d1.DMXMC, a.JZZDSM)), zy as (SELECT a.jzlsh, COALESCE(d1.DMXMC, b.CYZD) as label FROM cen_core_v30.TB_YL_ZY_Medical_Record a LEFT JOIN cen_core_v30.TB_CIS_LeaveHospital_Summary b ON (a.YLJGDM = b.YLJGDM AND a.jzlsh = b.jzlsh and b.xgbz = '1') INNER JOIN basic ON (a.kh = basic.kh AND a.klx = basic.klx) LEFT JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT DMXDMZ, DMXMC FROM cen_dic.tb_dic_disease) d1 ON b.CYZD = d1.DMXDMZ WHERE a.xgbz = '1' AND NOT REGEXP_LIKE(b.CYZD, '^(A|B|C|D[0-4][0-9]|F)') GROUP BY a.jzlsh, COALESCE(d1.DMXMC, b.CYZD)), cnt as (select label, jzlsh from mz union all select label, jzlsh from zy), jg as (select label, count(distinct jzlsh) as cs, row_number() over(order by count(distinct jzlsh) desc) rn from cnt group by label) select label, cs from jg where rn <= 10 order by rn desc
gms33grvk9buuDELETE FROM CEN_DOC_JYGL.TB_JM_0022 J WHERE J.ID IN (SELECT /*+ PARALLEL (a, 16) */ A.ID FROM CEN_DOC_JYGL.TB_JM_0022 A JOIN CEN_CORE_JYGL.T_SQZG_GPQY_QYXX B ON A.JM_ZJHM=B.SFZH WHERE B.QYSTATE<>'1' AND A.RWID='1' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM CEN_DOC_JYGL.V_JM_0022_RWID1 M WHERE A.GP_GH = M.GP_GH AND A.JM_ZJHM = M.JM_ZJHM AND A.RWID = M.RWID) )
gtvjzb2dtm3wwbegin MERGE INTO CEN_CORE_V30.TB_CIS_PRESCRIPTION_DETAIL a USING (SELECT CYH, CFMXH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, JZLSH, CXBZ, KH, KLX, JZKSDM, KFYS, KFYSXM, KFYSSFZ, KFRQ, CFTS, YZZH, XMMC, XMBMYB, CFLX, JXDM, JXMC, YPGG, YF, YPYF, YPSL, YPDW, SYPCDM, SYPC, JL, DW, YYTS, JYDM, SFPS, JCBW, BZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, jlscrq, posid FROM cen_interface_v30.TB_CIS_PRESCRIPTION_DETAIL c WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id ) b ON (A.YLJGDM=B.YLJGDM and A.CFMXH=B.CFMXH and A.CYH=B.CYH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET a.WSJGDM= b.WSJGDM, a.FJ_WSJGDM= b.FJ_WSJGDM, a.JZLSH= b.JZLSH, a.CXBZ= b.CXBZ, a.KH= b.KH, a.KLX= b.KLX, a.JZKSDM= b.JZKSDM, a.KFYS= b.KFYS, a.KFYSXM= b.KFYSXM, a.KFYSSFZ= b.KFYSSFZ, a.KFRQ= b.KFRQ, a.CFTS= b.CFTS, a.YZZH= b.YZZH, a.XMMC= b.XMMC, a.XMBMYB= b.XMBMYB, a.CFLX= b.CFLX, a.JXDM= b.JXDM, a.JXMC= b.JXMC, a.YPGG= b.YPGG, a.YF= b.YF, a.YPYF= b.YPYF, a.YPSL= b.YPSL, a.YPDW= b.YPDW, a.SYPCDM= b.SYPCDM, a.SYPC= b.SYPC, a.JL= b.JL, a.DW= b.DW, a.YYTS= b.YYTS, a.JYDM= b.JYDM, a.SFPS= b.SFPS, a.JCBW= b.JCBW, a.BZ= b.BZ, a.YLYL1= b.YLYL1, a.YLYL2= b.YLYL2 , a.jlscrq= b.jlscrq, a.posid= b.posid, a.jlgxsj = sysdate WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT(CYH, CFMXH, WSJGDM, FJ_WSJGDM, YLJGDM, JZLSH, CXBZ, KH, KLX, JZKSDM, KFYS, KFYSXM, KFYSSFZ, KFRQ, CFTS, YZZH, XMMC, XMBMYB, CFLX, JXDM, JXMC, YPGG, YF, YPYF, YPSL, YPDW, SYPCDM, SYPC, JL, DW, YYTS, JYDM, SFPS, JCBW, BZ, YLYL1, YLYL2, jlscrq, posid) VALUES( b.CYH, b.CFMXH, b.WSJGDM, b.FJ_WSJGDM, b.YLJGDM, b.JZLSH, b.CXBZ, b.KH, b.KLX, b.JZKSDM, b.KFYS, b.KFYSXM, b.KFYSSFZ, b.KFRQ, b.CFTS, b.YZZH, b.XMMC, b.XMBMYB, b.CFLX, b.JXDM, b.JXMC, b.YPGG, b.YF, b.YPYF, b.YPSL, b.YPDW, b.SYPCDM, b.SYPC, b.JL, b.DW, b.YYTS, b.JYDM, b.SFPS, b.JCBW, b.BZ, b.YLYL1, b.YLYL2, b.jlscrq, b.posid); DELETE FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_CIS_PRESCRIPTION_DETAIL b WHERE ROWID BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id; END;
gv3ppbaz8r16kBEGIN INSERT INTO cen_etl_v30.tmp_empi_02getnewdah (csdah, zjhm, zjlx, new_dah) SELECT a.csdah, a.zjhm, a.zjlx, nvl(MIN(b.dah), a.csdah) new_dah FROM cen_etl_v30.tmp_empi_01precsdah a LEFT JOIN cen_core_v30.tb_patient_information_x b ON (a.zjhm = b.zjhm AND a.zjlx = b.zjlx) WHERE 1 = 1 AND a.rowid BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id GROUP BY a.csdah, a.zjhm, a.zjlx; DELETE cen_etl_v30.tmp_empi_01precsdah a WHERE 1 = 1 AND a.rowid BETWEEN :start_id AND :end_id; END;

PGA Aggr Target Stats

  • B: Begin Snap E: End Snap (rows dentified with B or E contain data which is absolute i.e. not diffed over the interval)
  • Auto PGA Target - actual workarea memory target
  • W/A PGA Used - amount of memory used for all Workareas (manual + auto)
  • %PGA W/A Mem - percentage of PGA memory allocated to workareas
  • %Auto W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory controlled by Auto Mem Mgmt
  • %Man W/A Mem - percentage of workarea memory under manual control

Shared Pool Advisory

  • SP: Shared Pool Est LC: Estimated Library Cache Factr: Factor
  • Note there is often a 1:Many correlation between a single logical object in the Library Cache, and the physical number of memory objects associated with it. Therefore comparing the number of Lib Cache objects (e.g. in v$librarycache), with the number of Lib Cache Memory Objects is invalid.

Undo Segment Summary

Rule Set

  • Rule Sets ordered by Evaluations
  • * indicates Rule Set activity (re)started between Begin/End snaps

  • CPU(s),Ela(s) per Eval - avg CPU and Elapsed time per evaluation includes both SQL free and non-SQL free evals

Resource Limit Stats

  • Only rows with Current or Maximum Utilization > 80% of Limit are shown
  • For "UNLIMITED" resources, rows whose Current or Maximum Utilization exceeds 2*Initial Allocation are shown
  • Ordered by Resource Name

Shared Servers Dispatchers

  • Ordered by %Busy, descending
  • Total Queued, Total Queue Wait and Avg Queue Wait are for dispatcher queue
  • Name suffixes: "(N)" - dispatcher started between begin and end snapshots "(R)" - dispatcher re-started between begin and end snapshots

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 43.95 active sessions, 77.58% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 27.5 active sessions, 48.55% of total activity.
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "7s1rd38q2qwbj". Additionally, investigate this statement for possible
      performance improvements. You can supplement the information given here
      with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 70% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
      Look at data given below and an ASH report for further performance
      improvements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was executed 582 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 168 seconds.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "enq: TX - row lock contention" in wait class
      "Application" accounted for 27% of the database time spent in processing
      the SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on
      the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 5fazzyb6hs9g6.
         call DBMS_PARALLEL_EXECUTE.RUN_INTERNAL_WORKER ( :0,:1 )

Finding 2: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is 14.27 active sessions, 25.18% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 14.26 active sessions, 25.18% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant time on Cluster waits. For example, the UPSERT statement
      with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" is responsible for 50% of Cluster wait
      during the analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 14.64 active sessions, 25.84% of total activity.


Finding 3: Buffer Busy - Hot Objects
Impact is 8.02 active sessions, 14.16% of total activity.
---------------------------------------------------------
Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
database time.

   No recommendations are available.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
      database time.
      Impact is 8.02 active sessions, 14.16% of total activity.
         Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on
         this instance.
         Impact is 14.26 active sessions, 25.18% of total activity.
            Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
            Impact is 14.64 active sessions, 25.84% of total activity.


Finding 4: Row Lock Waits
Impact is 7.61 active sessions, 13.43% of total activity.
---------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements were found waiting for row lock waits.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 2.02 active sessions, 3.57% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Significant row contention was detected in the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_OTHERS" with object ID
      4037103. Trace the cause of row contention in the application logic
      using the given blocked SQL.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037103.
   Rationale
      The SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was blocked on row locks.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2

   Recommendation 2: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 1.92 active sessions, 3.38% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------

Finding 6: PL/SQL Execution
Impact is 1.97 active sessions, 3.48% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
PL/SQL execution consumed significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.94 active sessions, 3.42% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS"
      of type "PACKAGE" and ID 7169. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      6937 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS" of type "PACKAGE" and ID
      7169.

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, .06% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_REFRESH.REFRESH" of type "PACKAGE"
      and ID 15401. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition
      information.
   Rationale
      Subprogam ID 1 of PL/SQL ID 0 spent 122 seconds in execution.


Finding 7: Undersized SGA
Impact is 1.55 active sessions, 2.73% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .18 active sessions, .32% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the SGA by setting the parameter "sga_target" to
      278528 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 12.81 active sessions, 22.62% of total activity.


Finding 8: Global Cache Congestion
Impact is .85 active sessions, 1.51% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Global Cache Service Processes (LMSn) in other instances were not processing
requests fast enough.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .85 active sessions, 1.51% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase throughput of the Global Cache Service (LMSn) processes.
      Increase the number of Global Cache Service processes by increasing the
      value of the parameter "gcs_server_processes". Alternatively, if the
      host is CPU bound consider increasing the OS priority of the Global
      Cache Service processes.
   Rationale
      The value of parameter "gcs_server_processes" was "4" during the
      analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
      instance.
      Impact is 14.26 active sessions, 25.18% of total activity.
         Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
         Impact is 14.64 active sessions, 25.84% of total activity.

Wait class "Concurrency" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Configuration" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Session connect and disconnect calls were not consuming significant database
time.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 38.06 active sessions, 69.75% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 19.77 active sessions, 36.22% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 97% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was executed 276 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 146 seconds.
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on
      the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 5fazzyb6hs9g6.
         call DBMS_PARALLEL_EXECUTE.RUN_INTERNAL_WORKER ( :0,:1 )

Finding 2: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is 14.48 active sessions, 26.52% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 14.48 active sessions, 26.52% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant time on Cluster waits. For example, the DELETE statement
      with SQL_ID "5pgj4q7n5hs0b" is responsible for 24% of Cluster wait
      during the analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 15.13 active sessions, 27.72% of total activity.

Finding 5: PL/SQL Execution
Impact is 1.51 active sessions, 2.77% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
PL/SQL execution consumed significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.49 active sessions, 2.72% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS"
      of type "PACKAGE" and ID 7169. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      5339 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS" of type "PACKAGE" and ID
      7169.

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, .05% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_REFRESH.REFRESH" of type "PACKAGE"
      and ID 15401. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition
      information.
   Rationale
      Subprogam ID 1 of PL/SQL ID 0 spent 102 seconds in execution.


Finding 6: Undersized SGA
Impact is 1.51 active sessions, 2.77% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .17 active sessions, .32% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the SGA by setting the parameter "sga_target" to
      278528 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 10.48 active sessions, 19.2% of total activity.


Finding 7: Global Cache Congestion
Impact is .95 active sessions, 1.74% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Global Cache Service Processes (LMSn) in other instances were not processing
requests fast enough.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .95 active sessions, 1.74% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase throughput of the Global Cache Service (LMSn) processes.
      Increase the number of Global Cache Service processes by increasing the
      value of the parameter "gcs_server_processes". Alternatively, if the
      host is CPU bound consider increasing the OS priority of the Global
      Cache Service processes.
   Rationale
      The value of parameter "gcs_server_processes" was "4" during the
      analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
      instance.
      Impact is 14.48 active sessions, 26.52% of total activity.
         Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
         Impact is 15.13 active sessions, 27.72% of total activity.

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 32.04 active sessions, 90.91% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 32.02 active sessions, 90.84% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the CREATE TABLE statement with SQL_ID
      "49xg3tbq5jnha".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 49xg3tbq5jnha.
         create table TB_JM_0022_TMP as SELECT * FROM
         TABLE(PKG_JTYS_DBRWSC.F_GET_DBRW_PARALLEL('1', CURSOR(SELECT /*+
         PARALLEL(T,32) */  * FROM V_QYDX_SS T)))
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 99% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 99% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 1% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      I/O and Cluster wait for TABLE "CEN_CORE_JYGL.T_SQZG_GPQY_QYXX" with
      object ID 5515930 consumed 12% of the database time spent on this SQL
      statement.


Finding 2: Undersized SGA
Impact is 13.98 active sessions, 39.66% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is 13.63 active sessions, 38.66% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------

      360448 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 20.69 active sessions, 58.7% of total activity.

Global Cache Transfer Statistics

  • Immediate (Immed) - Block Transfer NOT impacted by Remote Processing Delays
  • Busy (Busy) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote Contention
  • Congested (Congst) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote System Load

Back to Top

Interconnect Ping Latency Statistics

  • Ping latency of the roundtrip of a message from this instance to
  • target instances.
  • The target instance is identified by an instance number.
  • Average and standard deviation of ping latency is given in microseconds
  • for message sizes of 500 bytes and 8K.
  • Note that latency of a message from the instance to itself is used as
  • control, since message latency can include wait for CPU
Target Instance500B Ping CountAvg Latency 500B msgStddev 500B msg8K Ping CountAvg Latency 8K msgStddev 8K msg
